reason: The score and justifications are well-supported by the source material. The HP Wiki and Screen Rant both confirm the film reduces Lavender almost entirely to the Ron relationship, stripping her Divination enthusiasm, her Trelawney loyalty (daffodils scene), her Binky grief, and her broader social presence across years 3-5. The personality score (18/25) is reasonable - the film does preserve her most visible traits (Ron infatuation, jealousy, exclamatory behavior) but loses the texture. The low arc score (10/25) is justified: the film gives her no arc beyond the Ron subplot, whereas the book tracks her from Divination devotee in year 3 through DA member in year 5 to Battle of Hogwarts fighter. The narrative role score (13/25) is defensible given the film cuts her social barometer function and compresses her active agency in the Ron relationship. One minor caveat: the justification mentions 'Luna and Hermione in non-Ron contexts' petty behavior - the wiki confirms she mocked Luna's earrings in OotP, so that's accurate. The overall 66/100 is in the right ballpark for a character whose core plot function is preserved but whose multi-year personality depth is almost entirely absent from the films.
